channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Brussels hospital joins new evacuation plan for war or natural disasters
item.description: The project aims to prepare Belgium for medical evacuations during major events including military conflicts and natural disasters.

item.title: Brussels to get a second Car-Free Sunday next year despite date row
item.description: Brussels residents can count on a second Car-Free Sunday in 2027 – but a row has broken out over the proposed new date.

item.title: 'Undertourism'? Brussels residents feel city is underrated by visitors
item.description: While many European capitals are seeking to curb the influx of visitors, the majority of Brussels residents believe that their city is underrated.

item.title: Brussels brasserie to host street party with food under €2
item.description: If you're strapped for cash at the end of the month and need a budget dinner before payday, then one Brussels brasserie has just the ticket.

item.title: Ommegang, crossbows and Charles V: Brussels Renaissance Festival is back
item.description: For seven weeks, the city will celebrate European heritage and Brussels' Renaissance history with a host of events and activities.

item.title: Luxury gym David Lloyd to phase out Aspria brand after Belgian takeover
item.description: The acquisition of Aspria, announced at the end of February, marks a major step in David Lloyd’s European expansion strategy.

item.title: What to expect from Brussels Pride 2026
item.description: Rainbows, concerts and festivities will bring the capital to life this weekend to mark Brussels Pride. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: Brussels to consider fighting rising aircraft noise pollution in court
item.description: The regional government could take legal action to reduce the use of a flight path that has disrupted the sleep of local residents for months.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 15 - 17 May
item.description: Holy Emperor Charles V, the start of this summer's festival season, or the Master of Suspense – there's plenty going on in Brussels this weekend.

item.title: 'Historic high' in e-scooter use in Belgium due to fuel prices and strikes
item.description: The return of the warmer seasones usually leads to an increase in e-scooter and e-bike activity, but this "historic" rise is due to more than the weather.

item.title: Brussels' Metro 3 extension could take at least another 10 years to build
item.description: "We have to build cathedrals underground," said one of the construction contractors of the controversial Metro 3 project in a hearing.

item.title: Four-day weekend for Ascension: What is it and what's open in Belgium?
item.description: Today's bank holiday marks the start of a four-day weekend for many in Belgium, as the majority of employees will take the Friday off as well.

item.title: Union Saint-Gilloise 'confident' new stadium will be built
item.description: Since 2019, the club has been looking to build a new stadium that would notably allow them to host their own European matches.

item.title: Trading sun for Belgian seasons: Finding home far from the Caribbean
item.description: “I know there is a growing community in Brussels, but it is still a minority."

item.title: STIB announces Brussels travel disruptions due to Belgian Cup final
item.description: With both sets of fans set to travel in numbers from the south of the city up to Heysel, there will be several disruptions throughout the day.

item.title: Brussels mayors consider taking 'discriminatory' police zone merger to court
item.description: "In the rest of the country, police zones are encouraged to merge. But that happens on a voluntary basis. In Brussels, this merger is being imposed."

item.title: Brussels hosts 'United for Palestine' concert as Eurovision alternative
item.description: Several NGOs are organising a concert on Tuesday in response to calls for a Eurovision boycott. "We refuse to accept double standards."

item.title: Man injured after being violently robbed in Saint-Gilles
item.description: A man was injured in a violent robbery in the Brussels municipality of Saint-Gilles, the local police zone in the Brussels-Midi area.

item.title: Shootings, floods or terrorist attacks: Are Brussels' EU institutions ready for a crisis?
item.description: Brussels' regular chain of command is complex enough. But should an emergency happen at the EU institutions, there are "some extra layers of complication".

item.title: Only one in three Brussels bars still serve small bowl of nuts with beer
item.description: The tradition of getting a little bowl of free peanuts when ordering an alcoholic drink – particularly a strong beer – in Brussels' bars is slowly disappearing.
